item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot about this that just doesn't engage me as a reader, but the most egregious parts, in-universe, have got to be the idea that the Foundation would allow D-class to go unsupervised in/with an anomaly for 6 hours, just because they were sleeping (allegedly sleeping, considering the recorders were off), and that they would allow the D-class to spend so much time in leisure activities with an anomalous subject, to get him open to talking, I guess, without encouraging them to ask more questions, sooner, or to investigate the cabin itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall, I think this is pretty solid, but I think it could use a couple adjustments. I'd add some statistics about SCP-3833-1 like a temperature for how cold it gets, and exactly how likely equipment failure is. Also for me, the audiovisual excerpts seem a little off. I think the D-Class are a little too happy a little to fast, and I'd think they'd be a bit of resistance to DND. Also I think it drags in a little, I'd recommend maybe cutting it into multiple expeditions up. Still got a plus 1 from me though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good to see this up here. :D

Check the first interview, though, there's an XXXX and some other extraneous doodads hanging about D:

item.title: Update #2
item.content:encoded: At the suggestion of shaggydredlocks in chat, I've removed all of the extraneous data things in the log. Hopefully it's a bit more coherent now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aside from being a rare good end for class Ds, there isn't much that caught my interest. no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
they are to attempt to find the cliffface where SCP-3833-2 is located

clifffface

On 07/19/2018, another group of five D-class personnel were made to ascend Nanga Parbat, and encountered SCP-3833-1. The group was directed to [DATA REDACTED], where SCP-3833-2 was located. It was discovered that two new cabins were built next to SCP-3833-2, apparently for the residence of the original group sent up to SCP-3833-2. The D-Class sent to investigate were persuaded to remove their equipment and enter the cabins.

The updated dialogue bits are a little shifty but this is pretty cool. Left me wondering what the actual anomaly might be.

+yes

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded: At the suggestion of literally everybody, I've removed the last addendum. I probably won't have time for a while to look through the logs, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is nice, enough for me to upvote, but you really should remove that last addendum. It's not really adding anything and I think it's just trying to be creepy for the sake of being creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I didn't get what was happening in the last addendum either, which is what stopped me from voting.

The rest of it, the D-class joining with Russel and starting a community to protect people from the storms, I liked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Between the Russian Slav named Russel (a trained chef who makes those traditional Russian delicacies, hot chocolate and muffins), the D-Class who make jokes about Ohm, use "chasm" off-hand and play chess and D&D, and the agent with a last name that only exists for the "we can still hear you" joke, I found none of this realistic or engaging.

The anomaly is not terribly interesting - how does the Foundation even know that the weather on Nanga Parbat is anomalous? how do they determine where the boundaries of 3833 start and finish? Russel isn't characterised so much as given a few random backstory facts which don't add up to a motivation, and the dialogue rings entirely false throughout. The last addendum seems like an attempt to goose the danger, but doesn't appear to have any connection to anything (is it Russel attacking 2344?).

Sorry - this really didn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted because this was a warm, fuzzy, good feelsy skip. However, I think the format of the logs was a bit sloppy (what they qualified as extraneous seemed very convenient for your grasp on characterization and such), and the final collapsible adds absolutely nothing.

A very simple explanation of Russel's situation and the D-Class warming up to him and then integrating into his little helper village is enough to make up for this, though.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
After a few months of dealing with writer's block and IRL problems, here's my first skip in what seems like forever. Thanks to these cool people for critique:

  • ModernMajorGeneral
  • Modern_Erasmus
  • Kakroom
  • Tanhony
  • TL333s

Image Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nanga_Parbat_The_Killer_Mountain.jpg

Edit: I'd like to bring attention to a Chinese translation by KirkeStein, and a Portuguese translation by cauazinlindaum.
